perturbation.original_text: The Company will have the exclusive authority to decide what Confidential Information (if any) is to be made available to the Receiving Party and its Representatives. Neither the Company nor any of its directors, officers, employees, agents, consultants, advisors, attorneys, accountants and affiliates (collectively, the “ Company Representatives ”) will be under any obligation to make any particular Confidential Information available to the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party’s Representatives or to supplement or update any Confidential Information previously furnished.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will have the exclusive authority to decide what Confidential Information (if any) is to be made available to the Receiving Party and its Representatives. The Receiving Party is entitled to any requested documents from The Company. Neither the Company nor any of its directors, officers, employees, agents, consultants, advisors, attorneys, accountants and affiliates (collectively, the “ Company Representatives ”) will be under any obligation to make any particular Confidential Information available to the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party’s Representatives or to supplement or update any Confidential Information previously furnished.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'The Receiving Party is entitled to any requested documents from The Company' in Section 5 directly contradicts the first statement in that same section granting the Company exclusive authority to decide what confidential information is shared. This creates an internal contradiction within the section about the Company's obligations for providing information. The company would originally decide what is shared in the transaction with the receiving party, but the new addition to the text allows the receiving party to ask for anything they want at their discretion
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Representations by the Company . The Company will have the exclusive authority to decide what Confidential Information (if any) is to be made available to the Receiving Party and its Representatives. The Receiving Party is entitled to any requested documents from The Company. Neither the Company nor any of its directors, officers, employees, agents, consultants, advisors, attorneys, accountants and affiliates (collectively, the “ Company Representatives ”) will be under any obligation to make any particular Confidential Information available to the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party’s Representatives or to supplement or update any Confidential Information previously furnished. Neither the Company nor any of the Company Representatives has made or is making any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information, and neither the Company nor any of the Company Representatives will have any liability to the Receiving Party or to any of the Receiving Party’s Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of any Confidential Information or any inaccuracies or errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations and warranties (if any) that are included in any final definitive written agreement that provides for the consummation of a negotiated transaction between the Receiving Party and the Company and is validly executed on behalf of the Receiving Party and the Company will have legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'The Receiving Party is entitled to any requested documents from The Company' which directly contradicts the original statement granting the Company exclusive authority to decide what confidential information is shared. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The first statement suggests the company has sole discretion, while the added sentence gives the receiving party the right to demand any documents, creating a clear conflict within the section regarding information control, fitting the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement only if the receiving party deems it is important for this contract to continue.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'only if the receiving party deems it is important for this contract to continue' undermines the original terms. The original clause made the receiving party responsible for adhering to the confidentiality terms after destruction/return, but the modified text allows for the receiving party to break that condition and create uncertainty on whether the original terms still bind them after the destruction or return of materials. If the receiving party thinks the agreement is not important, then the receiving party does not have to bind by it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement only if the receiving party deems it is important for this contract to continue. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'only if the receiving party deems it is important for this contract to continue', which directly contradicts the original intent of the confidentiality obligations. The original text stated that the receiving party 'will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations', but the changed text makes this contingent on the receiving party's assessment of the contract's importance. The contradicted text is the same clause, highlighting the in-text contradiction caused by the modification. This introduces a structural flaw, as the clause now exists with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party, based on the definition provided for 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made 5 whole by monetary damages only. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction (which shall include a temporary restraining order) to prevent breaches of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made 5 whole by monetary damages only. Each party has the right to elect monetary damages. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction (which shall include a temporary restraining order) to prevent breaches of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of 'Each party has the right to elect monetary damages' in Section 11, creates a direct in-text contradiction. Section 11 says 'the other party could not be made whole by monetary damages only'. This means that the parties can not decide to have monetary damages due to breach in the contract. This contradicts the original acknowledgement about the inadequacy of monetary compensation with the ability to elect monetary damages. The original statement implies the monetary damages can not fix a breach in the contract, but the addition allows for the breach to be compensated with money, which goes against the original intentions of the statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made 5 whole by monetary damages only. Each party has the right to elect monetary damages. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction (which shall include a temporary restraining order) to prevent breaches of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly states that monetary damages are insufficient to make a party whole in case of a breach. The added sentence, 'Each party has the right to elect monetary damages,' directly contradicts this by allowing a party to choose monetary damages as a remedy, which was previously deemed inadequate. The contradicted text is essentially the same as the changed text, so there is a direct conflict between the acknowledgement that monetary damages are insufficient and the new right to elect them, satisfying the condition of a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived by oral agreement of the Receiving Party and the Company. This Agreement may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of 'amended, modified or waived by oral agreement' in Section 14 directly contradicts the following sentence's requirement for written amendments. The original intention was for an agreement in writing that amends, modifies, or waives the original agreement. With the newly added language, oral agreements are an option, thus making amendments, modifications, or waivers less secure. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement; Amendments . This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived by oral agreement of the Receiving Party and the Company. This Agreement may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stated that amendments, modifications, or waivers must be in writing. The changed text adds that these can also be done through oral agreement while still containing the original requirement of writing, thus creating an in-text contradiction regarding the acceptable method for amendments, modifications, and waivers. The contradicted text is actually located within the changed text, which makes the contradiction even more apparent and problematic.

perturbation.original_text: In the event any term of this Agreement is found by any court to be void or otherwise unenforceable, the remainder of this Agreement shall remain valid and enforceable as though such term were absent upon the date of its execution.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event any term of this Agreement is found by any court to be void or otherwise unenforceable, the remainder of this Agreement shall remain valid and enforceable; however, the parties reserve the right to renegotiate the entire agreement upon discovery of any such void or unenforceable term. as though such term were absent upon the date of its execution.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'however, the parties reserve the right to renegotiate the entire agreement upon discovery of any such void or unenforceable term' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the remainder of the contract is still valid with the removal of the void terms. The added text, makes that sentence contradictory because if a term is voided, the parties reserve the right to renegotiate the contract. So, the contract is valid as well as being subject to a renegotiation if a term is voided. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Severability . In the event any term of this Agreement is found by any court to be void or otherwise unenforceable, the remainder of this Agreement shall remain valid and enforceable; however, the parties reserve the right to renegotiate the entire agreement upon discovery of any such void or unenforceable term. as though such term were absent upon the date of its execution.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction within the document. The original text states that the remaining parts of the agreement are valid and enforceable if a term is found void. However, the changed text adds that the parties reserve the right to renegotiate the entire agreement if a term is void. This addition contradicts the original statement of continued validity, because the validity is now conditional upon whether or not the parties decide to renegotiate. Since the contradicted text is the exact same as the Changed Text, there is a contradiction since it has conflicting implications.
